extra.message: User-supplied 'username' and 'password' POST parameters are concatenated directly into a SQL query string passed to cursor.execute(). An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL (e.g., username: ' OR '1'='1'--) to bypass authentication or dump the database.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: User-supplied 'server' POST parameter is concatenated directly into a shell command passed to os.popen(). An attacker can inject arbitrary OS commands (e.g., server='127.0.0.1; cat /etc/passwd') to achieve remote code execution on the host.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: The 'file' query parameter is appended to a relative path prefix ('read/') with no sanitization. An attacker can supply '../server.py', '../../etc/passwd', etc. to read arbitrary files on the filesystem that the process has access to.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: The URL path captured by the StaticHandler regex (.*) is appended to 'static/' with no validation or normalization. An attacker can request '/static/../../server.py' or '/static/../../../etc/passwd' to read arbitrary files outside the static directory.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: The Tornado template uses '{% raw query %}' and '{% raw link %}' to render the user-supplied 'q' query parameter without HTML escaping. The server also explicitly sets 'X-XSS-Protection: 0' (server.py line 88) to disable the browser's built-in XSS filter, guaranteeing XSS execution. An attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript via the search query parameter.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The response header 'X-XSS-Protection' is explicitly set to '0', disabling the browser's built-in reflected XSS filter. Combined with the use of '{% raw %}' in search.html, this guarantees that reflected XSS payloads in the 'q' parameter will execute in all browsers.

Tornado application is started with 'debug': True. In debug mode, Tornado exposes detailed tracebacks and error messages in HTTP responses, which can leak source code paths, variable values, and internal application details to attackers.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: Multiple plaintext credentials are hardcoded directly in the application source (e.g., admin/admin, test-user/test@123, etc.) and inserted into the database at initialization. Passwords are stored in plaintext with no hashing, allowing anyone with database read access to obtain all credentials immediately.

cwe: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password

extra.message: The UploadHandler accepts any file type and writes it directly to /tmp/ using the original file extension from the attacker-controlled filename. There is no content-type validation, no file extension allowlist, and no content inspection. An attacker can upload executable scripts (.py, .sh, .php, etc.) to /tmp/.

cwe: CWE-434: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type

extra.message: The HTTP server binds to '0.0.0.0', listening on all network interfaces. This exposes the application to all network-accessible clients, including external networks, without any network-level access restriction.

The UsersHandler POST endpoint (login) and ServerHandler POST endpoint (ping) do not use Tornado's built-in CSRF protection (no xsrf_cookies setting enabled, no _xsrf token checked). Any page on any origin can silently submit forms to these endpoints on behalf of a logged-in user.
The password field uses type='text' instead of type='password'. This causes the password to be displayed in cleartext on screen and may be cached in browser history or autofill.
